page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 4 physical cores with 4 vCPUs (1 thread per core) and a 35 MB L3 cache. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps under a KVM hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that this instance delivers modest compute and memory performance. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests place the server in the bottom 25% of performance tiers, with scores of 1105.68 and 2931.94 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show a significant drop from 163.7 GB/sec for small cached blocks to 19.6 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, which is categorized in the bottom 10%. Geekbench scores also reflect lower-tier performance, with a single-core score of 920.0 and a multi-core score of 1996.0.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.se1ne.xlarge offers a high memory capacity relative to its compute resources, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU throughput. However, the tradeoff is lower processing speed and memory bandwidth. It is best suited for workloads such as caching layers, lightweight database operations, or memory-heavy applications with low CPU demands, rather than intensive data processing or high-throughput web serving.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. It operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps and contains no local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark data indicates modest processing capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling into the bottom 25% and 10% performance tiers, respectively. Large-block memory bandwidth is also limited, measuring 19.6 GB/sec. Qualitatively, this server offers high memory density relative to its compute resources, presenting a tradeoff of lower CPU performance for increased RAM capacity. It is best suited for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads such as caching, lightweight database operations, or basic memory-heavy applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring high RAM density and dedicated Intel processors for workloads with modest CPU performance requirements.
